---
title: "A Game Changer in My Data Science Journey: A Year of Self-Hosting with Proxmox VE"
date: 2025-10-01
categories: [data-science, self-hosting, proxmox, virtualization, home-lab]
description: How Proxmox VE transformed my approach to data science and self-hosting.
---

## Day One: How I Accidentally Became My Own IT Department

It started innocently enough: a dusty ex-lease desktop, a vague memory of someone online saying "Proxmox is awesome", and a dangerous amount of curiosity.

A year later, my home office looked like a low-budget science-fiction movie.

## Enter Proxmox: The Rabbit Hole Beckons

When I first installed Proxmox VE, I thought, "How hard could it be?"

Within days, evenings were spent spinning up LXC containers and virtual machines like a caffeinated DJ at a tech conference.

## From Hello World to Hello, World of Problems

Most of my early adventures centered on programming environments, data pipelines, and getting JupyterLab to cooperate. Soon, I was running media servers, exploring distributed databases, and learning hard lessons about networking.

## Lessons Learned (Or: How I Learned to Love Snapshots)

Snapshots became my safety net. Before every risky change, I took a snapshot, crossed my fingers, and experimented freely.

Through all the breaking and fixing, Proxmox became a cornerstone of my data science workflow: enabling rapid experiments, safer failures, and faster iteration.

## Final Thoughts

If you are thinking about building a home lab, do it. You will learn far more than expected about infrastructure, reliability, and your own debugging patience.

May your containers always start, your VMs stay calm, and your family never notice the 2am reboots.
